transcript.text: No, no, no.
Hi, Mara.
Hey, Mara, how are you?
I'm doing okay.
How are you guys?
Good, good.
Love to hear.
Happy Friday, everyone.
Yeah, thank you, you too.
I don't really want to smell it.
Ismail is technically off next week.
I think he started from now, so I'm not sure.
Anyway, we can get started without him.
The main announcement is just, I think you guys have already done this, just create the threads on the linear issues.
And then if you can also bookmark those linear or slack thread links in the bookmarks in the internal channel, please, so that we can just retrieve it real quick.
that out know No.
Myers.
J
I you guys have created the threads, but I didn't see the links in the bookmarks, so please make sure you do that.
And just a reminder, please fill out Andy's Airtable with all the deliverables, etc.
And yeah, on Monday, please create a new thread.
Sorry about the Friday to Monday thing, but on Mondays, you can create a new thread for the linear issues.
We'll track that throughout the week and then every Monday from then on.
So, yeah, that was pretty much it.
Mara, did you have any announcements?
I think the only thing would be any questions on the timekeeping sheets or anything.
Are you guys kind of on track with that?
If not, just a reminder to do that so that we can share with Marcel.
Yeah, I think all clear.
We should track the time of our deliverables in spreadsheet.
Yeah, I think he's looking for, and you probably...
We already know this, but he's looking for, like, day by day, kind of like hour by hour, what are you working on?
How long is it taking you?
Just to get a sense of what tasks take the most time.
I know I mentioned this too, but it's coming from a good place because the clients are doing so well and this team is operating so efficiently.

So it's not like a, it's not a scary thing, but it would be helpful just to have everyone else get a sense of how long certain tasks take, you know, what eats up most of our time, and then also what's really efficient.
I had a question.
Do you want us to, you want us to drop it as a sheet, right?
It doesn't matter.
guess whatever is most convenient, but I can pull together like a template if that would be helpful just to standardize.
But yeah, sheet was totally fine.
That might be easiest.
I'm just using, like, my calendar kind of to chunk the time, so.
That's a relatively simple, similar format.
I'll do that and share it with you guys today.
Okay.
Sure.
Great.
All right.
Yeah.
Anything else you guys wanted to bring up?
Any issues, any observations over the past week?
Any blockers you've been having?
I know internal linking is still not working, but yeah.
Anything else?

Vivek, I wanted to talk about, because I'm doing extra piece publishing right now, and regarding the images, I've had to, especially for the plugin article, the one I added into the article when I converted the file into Markdown, it just kind of messed up the entire thing.
So I had to, I think, I checked the CMS and just checked the existing image format, and I saw that they used a Dropbox link.
So I've had to do that, and I'm having a bit of luck with that, basically.
That just,
An update.
Okay, so they're basically putting the links in a separate Dropbox folder or like how is that?
They upload the images they want to use in Dropbox, then they get the link, the Dropbox link, then they just convert that into like a markdown thingy and they just put it in the CMS.
But it's a very serious process actually, I don't know how WebEx would automate that moving forward.
Okay, that definitely should be automated.
So I think your, the SOP video that you sent them, I would maybe add a second video to it, just with this image process because that's a really important thing.
So, yeah.
Okay.
So we are currently doing something like that for Rapid, like convert the images to a link and get it automatically embedded in the XML file.
I think our client should be able to like help you with that.
Thank you.
Okay, good to know, which reminds me, Ismail, please do send over that video so that...
Oh, yeah, so I'm the...
We don't have any article in publishing queue.
Oh, man.
Okay.
We'll definitely have some for next week, so I'm not sure.
Is there a way you can...
You don't have to demo it, but is there a way to do, like, dry run or something or overview of the process?
Okay, think I can reduce the ones we've sent so far.
I can just speak for hours of time and we do the process.
Yeah, just on a high level so that next Friday I don't have to, you know, try to do the whole thing from scratch.
yeah.
All right.
Anything else, guys?
Anything else you wanted to point out?
Vivek, you mentioned the external links previously.
When Claude gives you the list of URLs, are they...
Generally aligned with the anchors that we're stating, for example, the payment takes like from three to five business days.
Should I check that statement on the links or should I just, what was the tendency of LLM there?
It gives you, so you can choose your anchor text, right?
So it will tell you, first of all, it will only give you internal links.
So if you're doing it for, I forgot, you're doing this for Rapid, right?
So if you're doing internal linking for Rapid, it will give you Rapid's log links and those, and it will suggest some anchor text.
You can take those suggestions.
So there's no real checking of the link as such.
I think most of the links are fine.
Timmy mentioned he got a few 404s, but I did it yesterday for Rapid on that one article.
I didn't see any.
So click through them and check it out.
But for the external links, links to studies and all that stuff, I think you need to use perplexity for it.
And
For that, you will need to check whether the link is valid or not.
But again, I would just be smart with the links you're searching for.
So search for something very specific.
So like I think that article you'd mentioned the central bank of whatever, this country and that country.
You know, search for something specific and payment networks.
The problem comes when you search for particular statistics, like 85% of this or whatever.
or like it just throws up something bonus.
But try to avoid that.
It should be fine.
Yeah, we're generally avoiding percentages without external links.
Yeah.
All right.
I think that was it from my end.
Anything else you guys want to bring up?
Ismail is not going on vacation despite taking time off.
So that's a negative mark against him.
I just want have a little shower, Ismail.
No, no.
No, I'm not traveling.
I'm actually staying in office, actually.
Okay.
I'm just taking some time off to go through a lot of resources that I wanted to go through.
Did you propose your future life?
Not yet.
Come on.
I'm going to give you a stop when that's happening.
I didn't know that was in the works, but all right.
Oh, okay.
Nika just disclosed sensitive information.
I was going to tell you guys anyway, too.
nothing.
All right.
Cool.
So the plan is to work even more when you're on your time off.
All right.
If that's pretty much it, I think we can end.
So, yeah.
All right.
Thanks, guys.
Nice to see all of you.
Enjoy your time, everyone.
Yeah, thank you.
Bye.
Bye.
Happy weekend.
Bye, guys.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly catch-up to discuss updates, challenges, and processes for the GrowthX Labs pod.](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [New process: Create threads on Linear issues, bookmark in Slack, fill Andy's Airtable with deliverables](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=40.0)
  - [Timekeeping sheets needed: Day-by-day, hour-by-hour breakdown of tasks for efficiency analysis](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=94.0)
  - [Image handling in CMS needs improvement: Current process uses Dropbox links, automation opportunity identified](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=226.0)
  - [Internal linking process clarified: Use Claude for internal links, Perplexity for external links with careful verification](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)

## Topics

### Linear Issue Tracking

  - [Create threads on Linear issues](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=40.0)
  - [Bookmark Slack thread links in internal channel for quick retrieval](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=47.0)
  - [Start new threads on Mondays for weekly tracking](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=73.0)
  - [Fill out Andy's Airtable with all deliverables](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=66.0)

### Timekeeping Process

  - [Detailed timesheets required: day-by-day, hour-by-hour breakdown](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=94.0)
  - [Purpose: Understand task duration, identify time-consuming vs. efficient processes](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=129.0)
  - [Mara to provide standardized template for consistency](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=164.0)
  - [Sheet format preferred for simplicity](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=174.0)

### CMS Image Handling

  - [Current process: Upload images to Dropbox, convert link to Markdown, insert in CMS](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=226.0)
  - [Identified as inefficient, automation opportunity](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=262.0)
  - [Timilehin to create SOP video for image process](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=271.0)
  - [Ismail mentioned similar process for Rapid, potential for cross-team learning](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=288.0)

### Internal Linking Process

  - [Use Claude for internal links (e.g., Rapid's blog links)](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)
  - [Verify suggested anchor text and check for 404 errors](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=403.0)
  - [Use Perplexity for external links, focusing on specific searches](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=413.0)
  - [Avoid using percentage statistics without external links](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=442.0)

### Publishing Queue

  - [Ismail: No current articles in publishing queue](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=309.0)
  - [Plan to do a dry run/overview of the process for next week's articles](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=323.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Create and bookmark Linear issue threads in Slack](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=40.0)
  - [Complete timekeeping sheets using Mara's upcoming template](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=94.0)
  - [Timilehin: Create SOP video for image handling process in CMS](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=271.0)
  - [Ismail: Prepare overview of publishing process for next meeting](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=323.0)
  - [Continue refining internal and external linking processes](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)
  - [Team to remain vigilant about proper link verification and anchor text selection](https://fathom.video/share/A-p4EApQuaPcqhUxJBK98af3tp5jnvyd?tab=summary&timestamp=403.0)

action_items.description: Bookmark Linear/Slack thread links in internal channel bookmarks for quick retrieval
Bookmark Linear/Slack thread links in internal channel bookmarks for quick retrieval
Create new thread for Linear issues on Monday, track throughout week
Create new thread for Linear issues on Monday, track throughout week
Create & share template for timekeeping sheets (day-by-day, hour-by-hour task tracking)
Add second SOP video for ExtraPiece image process (Dropbox link conversion to markdown)
Record & send video overview of Rapid publishing process to Vivek
